source_jobs.title: Sales Representative
source_jobs.description:
Location: Tampa, FL (Hybrid)

About Our Client

Our client is a global leader in sports and live event experiences, producing hundreds of world-class events across multiple brands and international markets each year.

With a collaborative culture, entrepreneurial mindset, and ambitious growth strategy, this is an opportunity to join a high-performing team focused on developing innovative partnerships with some of the world's most recognized brands.

About the Opportunity

 - We're seeking a highly motivated Sales Representative to join a growing commercial sales team.

 - This is a true hunter role focused on developing new business and creating strategic partnerships with leading national and global brands.

 - Success in this position requires someone who enjoys opening doors, building relationships from scratch, navigating complex sales cycles, and creating customized partnership solutions that deliver value for both the client and the organization.

 - If you're energized by outbound prospecting, thrive on building pipeline, and enjoy the challenge of turning "no" into "yes," we'd love to hear from you.

What You'll Do

 - Identify, prospect and develop new partnership opportunities across assigned industry categories

 - Build and manage a healthy pipeline of qualified prospects

 - Develop creative sponsorship and marketing partnership solutions aligned with client objectives

 - Lead discovery meetings and present customized partnership proposals

 - Negotiate and close new partnership agreements

 - Collaborate with internal marketing, events, digital and commercial teams to develop compelling partnership opportunities

 - Represent the organization at industry events, races, conferences and client meetings

 - Meet and exceed new business revenue goals

What We're Looking For

 - 2–5 years of quota-carrying B2B consultative sales experience

 - Demonstrated success in outbound prospecting and new business development

 - Track record of consistently meeting or exceeding sales targets

 - Experience managing multiple opportunities through a structured sales pipeline

 - Strong presentation and relationship-building skills

We're looking for someone who is:

 - Naturally curious and asks great questions

 - Resilient and comfortable hearing "no"

 - Highly competitive while remaining collaborative

 - Disciplined in outbound prospecting

 - Organized and process-driven

 - Creative in solving client business challenges

 - Passionate about sports and live events

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment

Skills

 - Microsoft Office

 - CRM experience (Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics, HubSpot, KORE or similar)

 - Strong written and verbal communication

 - Excellent presentation skills

 - Ability to manage multiple priorities

Location & Travel

 - Hybrid position based in Tampa, Florida

 - Candidates must be willing to work 3 days a week from the Tampa office.

 - Approximately 40–50% travel for client meetings, events and hosted experiences.

Compensation

Base Salary: $70,000

On-Target Earnings: Approximately $140,000

source_jobs.title: Business Development, Partnerships Consultant (HiveHealth)
source_jobs.description:
BD Partnerships Consultant (Sales/Relationship Representative, Contract, Remote, ASAP)



About the Role:

We are seeking a seasoned Sales/Relationship Representative to join our mission driven Hive Enterprise team on a contract basis. This is a fully remote position with an immediate start, ideal for a self-driven professional who thrives in managing their processes autonomously, building strong client relationships, and closing deals effectively.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Independently manage and prioritize a robust sales pipeline to consistently drive opportunities forward for both HiveSMART & HiveHEALTH offerings.

 - Build and maintain trusted relationships with clients to ensure long-term satisfaction and repeat business.

 - Close deals efficiently by tailoring strategies to client needs and overcoming objections with clear communication of complex value propositions persuasively to diverse stakeholders/decision makers.

 - Negotiate high-level SOW requirements to achieve mutually beneficial outcomes.

 - Note: you will have the partnership of Hive operations/legal on the formal contract phase. You will be equipped with SOW templates to run autonomously up to said stage.

 - Adapt quickly to changing market conditions and client requirements, solving problems proactively.

 - Track sales performance metrics and report progress regularly to leadership.



What We’re Looking For:

 - Proven track record of success in closing deals and managing client relationships in a sales or business development role.

 - Strong ability to work autonomously with minimal supervision while maintaining high productivity and motivation.

 - Excellent communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to influence decision-makers.

 - Demonstrated adaptability and problem-solving skills in dynamic environments.

 - Results-oriented mindset with experience meeting or exceeding sales targets.

 - Comfortable working remotely with reliable internet and a proactive approach to collaboration.



Contract Details:

 - Contract position with flexible hours

 - Remote work from anywhere

 - Immediate availability preferred



Why Join Us?

 - Opportunity to make a direct impact in a fast-paced, growing company

 - Work with a supportive, mission-driven team

 - Flexible work environment that values autonomy and results

source_jobs.title: Senior HR Business Partner
source_jobs.description:
We are partnering with a global events organization to appoint a Senior HR Business Partner supporting leaders across the EMEA region.

This is a high-impact, strategic role within a dynamic, internationally distributed People team, offering the opportunity to influence business outcomes through people strategy while remaining close to day-to-day operations.



Summary

The Senior HR Business Partner serves as a trusted advisor to senior leaders across EMEA, aligning people strategies with business objectives in a fast-paced, globally connected environment.

This individual will play a critical role in shaping the employee experience, organizational effectiveness, and talent strategy across multiple countries, operating in a lean, hands-on HR environment.



Responsibilities

Strategic Business Partnership

 - Partner closely with senior leaders across EMEA to align people strategy with business goals

 - Provide data-driven insights to support workforce planning, organizational design, and talent decisions

 - Act as a trusted advisor on performance, engagement, and retention strategies

Workforce Planning & Growth

 - Support long-term workforce planning and organizational effectiveness across the region

 - Identify opportunities to drive efficiency and enable scalable growth

 - Partner with leadership on evolving team structures and capability needs

Change Management

 - Lead and support organizational change initiatives across EMEA

 - Guide leaders through periods of transformation with a focus on communication, engagement, and culture

Employee Experience & Culture

 - Champion a positive, inclusive, and high-performance culture

 - Develop and implement initiatives to enhance employee engagement and retention

 - Gather and interpret employee feedback to inform continuous improvement

Talent & Performance

 - Partner with leaders on talent development, succession planning, and capability building

 - Support performance management processes including goal setting, feedback, and development planning

Employee Relations

 - Provide expert guidance on employee relations matters across multiple EMEA countries

 - Lead investigations and support resolution of complex workplace issues

 - Ensure fair, consistent, and compliant people practices across jurisdictions

Compensation & Reward

 - Advise on job leveling, compensation decisions, and market alignment

 - Support salary reviews, promotions, and incentive programs

People Operations & Compliance

 - Ensure compliance with local labor laws and internal policies across EMEA

 - Partner with People Operations on HR systems, payroll, and process improvements

 - Support ongoing development of people processes and systems



Key Requirements

 - Proven experience as an HR Business Partner supporting senior or executive leaders

 - Strong knowledge of employee relations across Europe / EMEA

 - Experience working in international, matrixed organizations

 - Demonstrated ability to operate in fast-paced, evolving environments

 - Strong relationship-building, influencing, and communication skills

 - Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution

 - Experience supporting growth, transformation, or organizational change



Additional Information

 - This is a regional role focused on EMEA, supporting multiple countries and leadership teams

 - The role operates within a lean HR structure, requiring breadth and adaptability

 - Travel across the region may be required

 - Compensation budgeted at £90,000 - £100,000 salary + Bonus + LTI



Why This Role?

This opportunity offers the chance to:

 - Work within a globally recognized, purpose-driven organization

 - Partner directly with senior leadership on impactful business decisions

 - Operate in a high-visibility, high-impact HR role

 - Contribute to the evolution of a regional People strategy within a global business



*This search is being conducted on a confidential basis. Further details will be shared

notes: No tech roles posted, mostly sales and HR positions
